title: Scientists found 10,000 possible exoplanets hiding in NASA data
summary: Scientists discovered 10,091 potential exoplanets in existing NASA data, requiring further confirmation. This significantly expands the pool of candidate worlds for future study.

title: SpaceX just fired up its 33-engine Starship 'V3' Super Heavy rocket booster. When could it fly? (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully test-fired all 33 engines of the Super Heavy booster for its Starship 'V3' rocket at its Texas facility. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the next Starship test flight.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts saw flashes on the far side of the moon that cameras struggle to capture. Here's why scientists are excited
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts observed impact flashes on the far side of the moon during their April 6 flyby, which has excited scientists due to the challenges in capturing such events. The observations are significant for future lunar missions and scientific research.

title: A bizarre 'decapitated' asteroid likely made the moon's largest impact crater. NASA's Artemis astronauts may land near the proof
summary: An ancient impact likely created the moon's largest crater and scattered deep lunar material toward NASA's Artemis landing sites. This discovery could influence where Artemis astronauts land near the lunar south pole.

title: A 29-pound Mars rock held NASA's Curiosity rover hostage for 6 days (video)
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover was temporarily immobilized for six days by a small Mars rock nicknamed 'Atacama.' The incident highlights the challenges of operating rovers on the Red Planet.

title: 'Saros' nails the cosmic horror and bullet-hell action assignments, but it falls short as a roguelite
summary: Saros delivers intense cosmic horror and bullet-hell action but struggles as a roguelite. The PlayStation and Housemarque follow-up to Returnal showcases PS5's capabilities while feeling internally inconsistent.

title: Where does Earth's mysterious 'ring current' come from? NASA and the US Space Force are launching a mission to find out
summary: NASA and the U.S. Space Force are launching a mission to study Earth's mysterious 'ring current'. The space weather mission aims to uncover the origins of this strange phenomenon surrounding our planet.

title: 'Star Wars: Galactic Racer': Release date, trailers, gameplay & everything we know about the return of podracing
summary: A new game titled 'Star Wars: Galactic Racer' is set to revive the iconic podracing concept from Star Wars. Details about its release date, trailers, and gameplay have been announced.

title: Moon joy at Jupiter | Space photo of the day for May 7, 2026
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a photo of Jupiter's moon Thebe. This image is featured as the space photo of the day for May 7, 2026.

title: Here's what 6 years of driving on Mars did to the wheels on NASA's Curiosity rover (video)
summary: A new NASA/JPL video documents six years of wear and tear on the wheels of the Curiosity rover during its driving on Mars. The footage highlights the durability and challenges faced by the rover's mobility system on the Red Planet.

title: Tracy Arm’s Post-Tsunami Landscape
summary: A landslide-triggered tsunami in summer 2025 stripped vegetation from the shore of Tracy Arm, a glacial fjord. This event highlights the environmental impact of natural disasters in glacial regions.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 supersonic jets and a Pilatus PC-12 utility plane, to support science and aeronautics missions. The center continues the legacy of flight research pioneers like Neil Armstrong.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords during a ceremony in Asunción, joining a coalition of nations committed to civil space exploration principles. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Paraguay's participation.

title: NASA Sends Mars Helicopter Blades Beyond Mach 1
summary: NASA tested next-generation Mars helicopter rotor blades in a simulator, finding they could exceed the speed of sound without damage. The experiment was conducted at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California.

title: NASA’s Next-Gen Mars Helicopter Rotors Are Moving Fast
summary: NASA is testing next-generation Mars helicopter rotor blades at supersonic speeds in the 25-Foot Space Simulator at JPL. The tests aim to advance rotor technology for future Mars missions.

title: NASA Pushes Next-Gen Mars Helicopter Rotor Blades Past Mach 1
summary: NASA's next-generation Mars helicopter rotor blades achieved supersonic speeds during tests at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory. The blades were tested in a chamber simulating Martian conditions.

title: Industry Moon Lander Training Cabin Lands at NASA for Artemis
summary: A full-scale mock-up of Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 2 crew cabin for NASA's Artemis program has been delivered for training and testing. The cabin will be used for mission simulations as NASA prepares to dock with lunar landers in Earth orbit.

title: A Light in the Dark
summary: The image shows a thin sliver of Earth's edge illuminated against the darkness of space, captured during the Artemis II mission on April 3, 2026. Artemis II was NASA's first crewed flight to test deep space capabilities, preparing for future lunar surface missions.

title: NASA-Supported Small Spacecraft Launches to Study Solar Particles
summary: A NASA-supported university-designed small spacecraft named SNAPPY was launched to study solar neutrinos. The mission took place aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Launch Complex.

title: NASA’s Prithvi Becomes First AI Geospatial Foundation Model In Orbit
summary: NASA and IBM have successfully demonstrated the Prithvi Geospatial AI foundation model in orbit, marking the first instance of such a model being used on in-orbit platforms. This achievement highlights advancements in AI-driven geospatial analysis from space.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also progressing in deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is being converted and upgraded after the completion of Flight 11, with Flight 12 nearing readiness. The changes aim to support future missions as part of ongoing improvements.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 flight, potentially marking a brief period of calm before an upcoming surge in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, expanding access to space for multiple customers.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, with an updated trajectory plan. The company has issued notices for the upcoming launch window.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an ISS mission. The upcoming mission is discussed in detail.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks the rocket's return to flight after a long pause.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for Artemis III has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II mission, which recently launched four astronauts on a lunar flyby.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return as part of the scheduled launches.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry topic. No further details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute bake-out for a space mission. This process ensures the parachute's proper deployment and functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications. This innovation aims to improve the handling and servicing of spacecraft in orbit.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process for 2026, including an in-person SME Forum in Paris and an online preparatory session.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received. The location for this achievement is highlighted as optimal.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round, unveiling a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for lunar exploration.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab is set to acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, which will be rebranded as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company's S-1 filing in May indicated a target range of $24-$26 per share.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, emphasizing thermal imaging as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to fill a gap in existing EO capabilities with its thermal imaging technology.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is advancing efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is positioning itself to capitalize on the growing global interest in quantum advancements.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar power, addressing vulnerabilities in fuel line deliveries. The company aims to eliminate strategic threats by harnessing solar energy in space.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a funding round, including a $300 million Series E led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation and scale its business. The funds will also be used to develop technology to meet increasing demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to position the OSC as a central hub for such applications.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, secured an additional $60 million in funding, making it India's first space 'unicorn'. The company is a key player in India's growing space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after securing significant funding.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites per year. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported 32% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 and over 40% growth in its satellite manufacturing segment, driven by projects for Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley stated that the Globalstar deal did not impact these results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission capabilities, resilience, and adoption of emerging technologies. The agency's new director emphasizes transformation and workforce development to meet evolving threats and warfighter needs.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.

title: Verizon Details ‘Satellite-Everywhere’ for Disaster Response, Expands Satellite Fleet
summary: Verizon is expanding its satellite fleet to enhance disaster response capabilities and aims for a 'satellite-everywhere' failover strategy. The company shared this plan with Via Satellite.

title: Astranis Raises $300M in Series E as Part of $450M in New Capital
summary: Astranis raised $300 million in a Series E funding round and secured an additional $155 million credit facility to boost satellite production. The total new capital amounts to $450 million.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration focuses on developing Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite technology.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has partnered with Impulse Space, Inversion Space, K2 Space, and others to develop space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award to prototype these systems.
